source_jobs.title: GTM Automation Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT HUBSTAFF & THE OPPORTUNITY


At Hubstaff, we help more than 140,000 global users improve productivity through workforce analytics, time tracking, and workforce management software. Our platform helps businesses track time, gain visibility into work, manage schedules, and automate payments with transparency and accountability at its core. We've been fully remote for over a decade and are building the tools that power the future of distributed work.

We're now investing heavily in AI, automation, and signal-driven go-to-market systems to accelerate growth beyond our traditional inbound motion.

This is a rare opportunity to become Hubstaff's first GTM Automation Engineer and build this function from the ground up.

Working closely with leaders across Marketing, Sales, Revenue Operations, and Customer Success, you'll design the automation, AI workflows, and GTM systems that power outbound growth, account-based marketing, and revenue generation.

If you enjoy building from scratch, experimenting with emerging AI technologies, and creating measurable business impact, you'll have the opportunity to shape both our growth strategy and the future of this function at Hubstaff.

 
 


WHAT YOU'LL DO



BUILD AND SCALE SIGNAL-BASED GTM SYSTEMS

 - Design and operationalize workflows that transform product usage, intent, hiring, firmographic, and behavioral signals into automated sales and marketing actions

 - Build repeatable systems that identify, prioritize, and engage target accounts at the right time

 - Create scalable triggers that improve targeting, personalization, and pipeline generation


OWN OUTBOUND AND ACCOUNT-BASED INFRASTRUCTURE

 - Build account identification, enrichment, scoring, segmentation, and orchestration systems

 - Improve outbound efficiency through automation, data enrichment, and intelligent targeting

 - Partner closely with Marketing and Sales to create scalable, always-on outbound motions


DESIGN AI-POWERED AUTOMATION WORKFLOWS

 - Build AI-assisted workflows for account research, prioritization, enrichment, routing, and personalization

 - Leverage tools such as Claude Code, AI Studio, ChatGPT, and emerging agent frameworks to increase GTM efficiency and effectiveness

 - Evaluate and implement new technologies that improve automation, targeting, and revenue generation


DRIVE GTM EXPERIMENTATION

 - Rapidly test new ICPs, messaging strategies, engagement channels, and orchestration models

 - Launch experiments, measure outcomes, and iterate based on performance

 - Help shape Hubstaff's long-term approach to AI-powered go-to-market execution

 
 


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR


MUST-HAVES

 - 5+ years of experience in GTM Engineering, Growth Operations, Revenue Operations, Marketing Operations, Sales Operations, or a similar technical GTM role

 - Experience building and deploying automation workflows that drive measurable business outcomes

 - Strong HubSpot experience, including workflows, integrations, custom objects, and automation architecture

 - Experience with AI-assisted workflows, automation platforms, and agent-based systems

 - Experience with outbound, ABM, demand generation, or revenue growth motions

 - Ability to connect APIs, systems, and data sources using low-code, no-code, or light-code solutions

 - Strong systems-thinking mindset and ability to work across Marketing, Sales, Success, Product, and Data teams

 - Excellent communication skills and comfort working with both technical and business stakeholders


NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience with Clay, Common Room, Apollo, 6sense, Lemlist, Outreach, Salesloft, or similar GTM platforms

 - Experience with agentic workflow tools such as Claude Code, n8n, AI Studio, or similar automation frameworks

 - Basic scripting experience in Python, JavaScript, SQL, or APIs

 - Experience in PLG SaaS or hybrid PLG + sales-assisted SaaS environments

Experience building GTM systems from scratch in startup or growth-stage companies


Compensation & B

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Engineer (Growth Team)
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR PRODUCT ENGINEER (GROWTH TEAM)


ABOUT HUBSTAFF

At Hubstaff, our mission is to empower people to have their most productive workday. We do this by providing the industry-leading workforce analytics and time tracking platform for remote and distributed teams.

We've been fully remote for over a decade—long before it became a trend—and we continue to believe that remote work represents the most significant shift in the modern workplace since the advent of the PC. Our platform helps more than 200,000 global users track time, gain visibility into work, manage schedules, and automate payments with transparency and accountability at its core.

We're a product-led company with a winning culture, strong momentum, and a long-term vision: to become an essential part of every remote company's tech stack. And we're just getting started.

 
 


THE ROLE

We're hiring a Senior Product Engineer to join our Growth Team at Hubstaff. This is a full-time (40 hrs/week), remote position with long-term potential.

The Growth Team sits at the intersection of Product, Design, Data, and Engineering. Our mission is to improve customer acquisition, activation, engagement, retention, and expansion through experimentation, rapid iteration, and continuous product improvement.

You'll play a key role in building customer-facing experiences that directly impact product adoption, user engagement, and business outcomes. Working closely with product managers, designers, developers, and data stakeholders, you'll help identify opportunities, ship improvements, and measure results.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys owning features end-to-end, working across both frontend and backend systems, collaborating closely with Product and Design, and connecting technical decisions to customer and business outcomes.

 
 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build customer-facing features using Ruby on Rails, PostgreSQL, and modern frontend technologies such as Vue.js, Hotwire, and Tailwind CSS.

 - Brainstorm, ideate, theorize and discuss solutions for achieving real business impact with small but high-impact product changes.

 - Implement experiments at speed, cutting scope ruthlessly, looking for the fastest way to answer the hypothesis.

 - Drive and own initiatives from idea through implementation to measurement, with license to make decisions that speed up or improve learning.

 - Work autonomously, but collaboratively, partnered with engineers, product and design, communicating proactively on risk and opportunities.

 - Employ metrics, A/B tests, fake doors, customer outreach, technology research and more to guide product and technical decisions.

 
 


WHAT YOU SHOULD HAVE

 - Strong professional experience with Ruby on Rails.

 - Strong PostgreSQL fundamentals.

 - Strong product mindset and customer empathy.

 - Ability to clearly explain the business impact of projects you've worked on.

 - High level of ownership, autonomy, and accountability.

 - A determination to build fast, ship soon and tweak later.

 - Experience building and maintaining customer-facing web applications.

 - Experience working across both frontend and backend systems.

 - Experience collaborating closely with Product, Design, and Engineering teams.

 - Experience working remotely and asynchronously with distributed teams.

 - Excellent communication skills in a remote-first environment.

 
 


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with Vue.js.

 - Experience with Hotwire (Turbo + Stimulus).

 - Tailwind CSS experience.

 - SaaS product experience.

 - Product analytics experience.

 - Experimentation tooling (e.g. statsig, customer.io http://customer.io)

 - Experience running and evaluating product experiments and A/B testing.

 - Experience using AI-assisted development tools.

 - Instinct for delivering business impact and outcome-oriented mindset.

 - Previous experience in startup or high-growth SaaS companies.

 
 


LOCATION

Hiring in: Argentina, Brazil, Poland, Romania, Spain, Portugal

source_jobs.title: Mid Graphic/Motion Designer (Part-Time, Remote — LATAM)
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY OVERVIEW

At Hubstaff, our mission is to empower people to have their most productive workday. We do this by providing the industry-leading workforce analytics and time tracking platform for remote and distributed teams. We've been fully remote for over a decade — long before it became a trend — and we continue to believe that remote work represents the most significant shift in the modern workplace since the advent of the PC. Our platform helps more than 140,000 global users track time, gain visibility into work, manage schedules, and automate payments with transparency and accountability at its core. We're a product-led company with a winning culture, strong momentum, and a long-term vision: to become an essential part of every remote company's tech stack. And we're just getting started.




ROLE OVERVIEW

We’re looking for a mid-level Graphic & Motion Designer who can bring ideas to life across motion, video, and digital design.

This role sits at the intersection of Marketing, Web, and UX, supporting product storytelling, website experiences, and performance-driven creative. You’ll work on a mix of product videos, motion for web, and conversion-focused assets, with a strong focus on quality, consistency, and iteration.

This is a part-time (≈20 hrs/week), fully remote role based in LATAM, with collaboration across global teams.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Create product videos and motion assets (walkthroughs, feature clips, explainers) from concept to final delivery.

 - Design web motion experiences (hero loops, micro-interactions, hover states) aligned with our design system.

 - Produce high-performing creatives for experiments (A/B tests across formats, durations, and messaging).

 - Deliver multi-format assets for web and paid channels, ensuring consistency across breakpoints and platforms.

 - Handle art finalization for digital deliverables (exports, formats, performance optimization, accessibility).

 - Collaborate with Web/Front-end teams on implementation (Lottie, SVG, motion specs).

 - Maintain organized libraries, templates, and reusable components.

 - QA assets across devices, browsers, and formats to ensure quality and performance.

 - Contribute to motion guidelines and system consistency.

 - Partner with Marketing to iterate based on performance insights (e.g., engagement, click-through).




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3–5 years of experience across motion design, video editing, and graphic design (agency or in-house).

 - A strong portfolio demonstrating:
   
   - Product or SaaS videos
   
   - Motion for web (micro-interactions, UI animations)
   
   - Social or ad creatives
   
   - Clean, modern design fundamentals

 - Hands-on experience with art finalization and production-ready assets.

 - Experience designing for web and performance marketing (multiple formats and aspect ratios).

 - Comfortable working cross-functionally with Marketing, UX, and Engineering.

 - Strong attention to detail, organization, and consistency.

 - Ability to work independently in a remote environment and manage priorities.




TOOLS & STACK

 - Motion & Video: After Effects, Premiere Pro (or DaVinci Resolve), Media Encoder

 - Animation: Lottie (Bodymovin), Rive, basic SVG animation

 - Design: Figma, Illustrator, Photoshop

 - Nice to have: Blender or Cinema 4D

 - Collaboration: Slack, Notion/Jira, Google Drive

 - Bonus: Basic HTML/CSS understanding for handoff




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in B2B SaaS or tech environments

 - Familiarity with A/B testing and performance-driven design

 - Basic analytics awareness (GA4, PostHog, or similar)

 - Experience with sound design, thumbnails, or 3D product visuals

 - Scripting knowledge (AE expressions, Rive logic, or similar)

 - Illustration skills




REQUIREMENTS

 - Fluent English (written and spoken) — you’ll collaborate across global teams, write specs, and join live reviews.

 - Based in LATAM, with availability overlapping UTC-5 (±2 hours).

 - Reliable inte

source_jobs.title: Product Manager, Growth
source_jobs.description:
PRODUCT MANAGER, GROWTH



ABOUT HUBSTAFF

At Hubstaff, our mission is to empower people to have their most productive workday. We do this by providing the industry-leading workforce analytics and time tracking platform for remote and distributed teams.

We've been fully remote for over a decade—long before it became a trend—and we continue to believe that remote work represents the most significant shift in the modern workplace since the advent of the PC. Our platform helps more than 200,000 global users track time, gain visibility into work, manage schedules, and automate payments with transparency and accountability at its core.

We're a product-led company with a winning culture, strong momentum, and a long-term vision: to become an essential part of every remote company's tech stack. And we're just getting started.

 
 


THE ROLE

We're hiring a Product Manager to join our Growth Team at Hubstaff. This is a full-time, remote position where you'll help shape the future of Hubstaff's product experience through data-driven experimentation and customer insights.

The Growth Team sits at the intersection of Product, Design, and Engineering. Our mission is to improve customer acquisition, activation, engagement, retention, and expansion through experimentation, rapid iteration, and continuous product improvement.

You'll own the Growth experimentation pipeline from idea through execution, using customer insights, business metrics, and product analytics to identify opportunities, prioritize experiments, and measure success. You'll partner closely with Engineering, Design, Marketing, and an embedded data analyst, but you'll be expected to drive decisions through your own analytical thinking and curiosity.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving ambiguous problems, digging into data, challenging assumptions, and turning insights into product experiments that create measurable business impact. If you're energized by experimentation, love validating ideas with evidence, and thrive in a fast-moving environment, you'll be right at home on the Growth Team.

 
 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the Growth experimentation pipeline from ideation through implementation and analysis.

 - Lead approximately 10–20 product experiments each quarter focused on activation, onboarding, engagement, and retention.

 - Develop hypotheses grounded in customer behavior and business metrics.

 - Define success metrics before experiments launch and evaluate results using quantitative and qualitative data.

 - Partner with an embedded data analyst to uncover insights while independently exploring data to identify new opportunities and validate ideas.

 - Translate data, customer feedback, and experiment learnings into product decisions and future roadmap recommendations.

 - Partner closely with Engineering, Design, and Marketing to shape, build, launch, and evaluate experiments.

 - Balance rapid experimentation with larger strategic initiatives that improve long-term product growth.

 - Talk regularly with customers to better understand their needs and identify new opportunities.

 - Communicate experiment progress, learnings, and recommendations clearly across the organization.

 
 


WHAT YOU SHOULD HAVE

 - Product Management experience within a SaaS environment.

 - Experience running multiple A/B tests or product experiments from hypothesis through analysis.

 - Exceptional analytical skills and a passion for using data to solve customer problems.

 - Experience defining success metrics, interpreting experiment results, and making product decisions based on evidence rather than opinion.

 - Ability to perform meaningful product analysis independently using customer behavior, business metrics, and product analytics.

 - Strong product sense with the ability to identify meaningful customer problems and propose thoughtful solutions.

 - Strong understanding of activation, onboarding, retention, engagement, or product-led growth concepts.

 - High

notes: Remote work platform with a strong focus on product development and growth.
